finding.title: CI workflow matches required triggers and quality gates

details.point: Triggers on push to main, push of v* tags, and PRs targeting main.
Runs formatting check (ruff format --check), lint (ruff check), and typing (mypy src/) before tests.
Uses astral-sh/setup-uv with Python 3.13 and dependency caching, then uv sync --frozen.

finding.title: Integration tests will run in CI and may be flaky due to Docker dependency

details.point: The test job runs uv run pytest (full suite). If integration tests spin up Docker containers, GitHub-hosted runners usually support Docker, but failures can occur due to network, image pull limits, or tracker connectivity.
This is aligned with the stated requirement to run the full suite, but expect occasional external flakiness unrelated to code.

recommendation.point: If flakiness becomes an issue, consider marking network-dependent tests with a separate marker and running them in a dedicated job with retries or increased timeouts, while still keeping them required for merge/release.

finding.title: Publish workflow correctly gates on CI success via workflow_run

details.point: Workflow triggers only after CI completes; each publish job checks conclusion == success.
Both jobs check out the exact triggering commit using head_sha, preventing publishing a different revision than was tested.

finding.title: Release publishing condition likely will not work for tag pushes

details.point: On workflow_run events triggered by tag pushes, head_branch is typically the source branch (e.g., main) or can be empty/null; the tag name is not reliably available as head_branch.
As a result, tagged releases may never publish semver tags, even though CI ran on tag push.

recommendation.point: Derive release intent and version from the tag ref instead of head_branch. Common patterns: 1) Trigger publish directly on push tags (v*) and gate on CI via required status checks (branch protection), or 2) Keep workflow_run but query the Git ref associated with head_sha (GitHub API) to find tags pointing to that commit, or 3) Use github.event.workflow_run.head_repository + head_sha and a git fetch --tags to detect tags containing HEAD, then compute version tags.
If staying with workflow_run, update the condition to detect a tag context and compute VERSION accordingly (not from head_branch).

finding.title: Docker image tagging logic is correct assuming version is derived correctly

details.point: The shell logic computes VERSION, MAJOR, and MINOR and pushes :x.y.z, :x.y, :x.
Build uses buildx, linux/amd64 only, and GHA cache for layers; aligns with requirements.
Uses docker/login-action with DOCKERHUB_USERNAME and DOCKERHUB_ACCESS_TOKEN secrets.

action.title: Fix release publish detection for v* tags

steps.step: Adjust publish-release job condition and version derivation so it reliably triggers on tag pushes.
Ensure VERSION is taken from the tag (e.g., v1.2.3), not from head_branch.
Add a quick validation note in the workflow (or a minimal test run) to confirm tags publish as expected.

action.title: Consider resilience for Docker/network-dependent integration tests

steps.step: Optionally split unit vs integration tests into separate jobs and apply retries/timeouts to integration job if needed.
